title: Turquoise
content:
A baby went to heaven while it slept,
         And, waking, missed its mother's arms, and wept.
    Those angel tear-drops, falling earthward through
         God's azure skies, into the turquoise grew.
meaning:
"Turquoise" is a quintessential example of Ella Wheeler Wilcox's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
